title: Pabstiella alligatorifera
taxonomy: Pabstiella alligatorifera is classified within the family Pleurothallidinae, which belongs to the subtribe Pleurothallidinae of the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This classification is based on its unique characteristics and morphological features. Pabstiella alligatorifera is further grouped within the genus Pabstiella, which is a part of the tribe Epidendreae. Within the genus, Pabstiella alligatorifera is classified as a species with a distinct set of characteristics that differentiate it from other species. The classification of Pabstiella alligatorifera is essential for its identification, conservation, and study in the field of orchidology.

intro: Pabstiella alligatorifera is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of South America, characterized by its unique epiphytic growth habit and delicate, white flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
